title: Fear Garden — Level 5 Senior Detective
description: LEVEL 5 SENIOR DETECTIVE CASE version of Fear Garden with unique suspect, method, motive, evidence, and answers.

answerKey.method: Used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame Trevor
answerKey.motive: To cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the Beer Garden expansion
answerKey.keyEvidence: Duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving trevor’s login was staged
answerKey.explanation: Lena Vale had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving Trevor’s login was staged with the failed statement: Lena said she only handled guest check-in.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Fear Garden begins at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This session is Level 5: Senior Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 5: Senior Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the body scene.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare the timeline before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital and venue systems now matter.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Andre Moss missed two calls from Lena Vale before the garden music cut out.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Andre moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Andre texted Lena: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Lena replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Andre’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Lena’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the beer garden expansion.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Lena.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Lena near the restricted service path before the incident.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: duplicate badge moves while Trevor is visible outside.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Andre disappears from frame.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Lena saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Andre saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Andre.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: A security export skips the exact Beer Garden patio window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata connects the edit to the suspect’s access trail: Lena Vale.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A browser search asks how to remove timestamp metadata from venue clips.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search matches the method: used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame trevor.

rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the body in the garden proves before the next round opens.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close Fear Garden.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before Fear Garden locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Fear Garden at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. You are playing Level 5: Senior Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 5: Senior Detective Case. The culprit was Lena Vale. The method: Used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame Trevor. The motive: To cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the Beer Garden expansion. The proof: Duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving trevor’s login was staged.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Andre’s death suspicious?

options.label: A normal spilled pint near the stage
duplicate badge moves while Trevor is visible outside
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
A guest paid with cash
Trevor’s login appears from Lena’s office terminal
A server dropped a tray outside

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect
shell lease ledger points to Lena’s account

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: keg-room pressure log was edited after the body was found
A birthday receipt
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Maya said the plants were watered
Lena said she only handled guest check-in
Janelle said the gate was busy
Darius said the speaker was loud
Trevor Knox
Janelle Price
Lena Vale
Darius Cole

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only
Staged a random parking-lot fall
Used a duplicate manager badge to stage a fake keg-room accident and frame Trevor
To cover up a shell-company lease scheme tied to the Beer Garden expansion
To win a trivia prize
To protect a karaoke score
To hide a family recipe

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: A guest rumor and a napkin
Duplicate badge audit, shell lease ledger, and metadata proving trevor’s login was staged
A broken patio chair only
A single missed call with no context

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which suspect statement falls apart?

options.label: Maya said the music was loud
Trevor said the patio was crowded
Lena said she only handled guest check-in
Janelle said the menu was changed
